search.placeholder: Search updates, tasks, controls or frameworks...

hero.subtitle: RegLens monitors regulatory sources, detects changes, maps to controls, and produces audit-ready evidence. Meet your enterprise RegTech needs with open source and free.
hero.footerNote: Registration and use are completely free. You can run it on your own server.

why.title: Why RegLens?
why.problemDesc: Compliance teams have to manually track dozens of sources; controls and evidence fall out of date, processes are fragmented.
why.solutionDesc: RegLens automates the full change-to-action flow from source monitoring to audit packs; one dashboard for metrics, risks, and tasks.

features.title: Compliance on one platform
features.subtitle: From source monitoring to audit packs, the full process with RegLens.
features.dashboardDesc: Real-time compliance metrics, risk distribution, task status, recent updates and critical tasks in one view.
features.trackSourcesDesc: Add RSS and API sources; follow regulators and standards from one place.
features.detectChangesDesc: Turn text into structured form with AI; classify new, updated and removed items.
features.mapControlsDesc: Automatic control mapping to frameworks like SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR, HIPAA; with confidence score.
features.generateTasksDesc: Auto-create tasks from mappings; team management with priority, assignment and due date.
features.collectEvidenceDesc: Create evidence templates for audit; link to tasks and controls, manage approval workflow.
features.auditPacksDesc: Bundle tasks and evidence in one pack; export as PDF, Excel, CSV.
features.aiSubtitle: Structure text, map to controls and get evidence suggestions with Mistral AI.
features.normalizeDesc: Turn raw regulatory text into title, summary, category and domain.
features.mapDesc: Automatically map changes to framework controls; with confidence score and rationale.
features.evidenceGenDesc: Create evidence templates and text suggestions for tasks and controls.
features.openSourceDesc: RegLens is fully open source. Use it without license restrictions, run it in your own environment and adapt it to your needs. We welcome community contributions.
features.openSourceLogin: Already have an account? Log in
features.securityDesc: Authentication, encrypted data, audit logs and industry-standard compliance.
features.faqFreeA: Yes. RegLens is open source and free to use. You can install and run it on your own server with no license restrictions.
features.faqDataA: Your data is protected with authentication, HTTPS, encryption and audit logs. The architecture aims for GDPR and SOC 2 / HIPAA compliance.
features.faqExportA: Yes. You can export audit packs as PDF, Excel and CSV. You have access to all your data in standard formats.
features.faqLangA: The interface is available in English, French, Spanish and Turkish. Regulatory content is processed in the source language; normalization and mapping are done with AI.
features.finalCtaSubtitle: With RegLens, monitor sources, map changes, manage tasks and produce audit evidence from one place.
features.footerTagline: Open source · Free to use · Continuous Regulatory Change-to-Action
features.footerSupport: Use GitHub Issues or community channels for support and contributions.

team.title: Team
team.description: Organization members. Admins invite by username; invites appear in Invites and as notifications.
team.pendingInvitesDescription: Invited users will see the invite on the Invites page and in notifications.
team.inviteMemberDescription: Search by username; the invited user will see the invite in notifications and on the Invites page.
team.userCanAcceptDecline: The user can accept or decline from the Invites page.

organizations.pageDescription: You can create multiple organizations or accept invites. The active organization (selected from the sidebar) determines data on all pages: sources, updates, tasks, charts and reports belong only to that organization. Changing organization keeps data separate.
organizations.noOrgsDescription: Create a new organization or accept an invite to continue.
organizations.newOrgDescription: When you create an organization you become an admin (ADMIN); you can then send team invites.
organizations.selectOrgDescription: Select the organization you want to use. It will determine data shown on all pages (dashboard, sources, updates, tasks, compliance, charts).

addFriend.title: Add Friend
addFriend.description: Search by username and send a friend request.
addFriend.chatFromMessages: You can chat with friends from the Messages page.

invites.title: Invites
invites.description: Team invites and friend requests sent to you; accept or decline.
invites.acceptDescription: When you accept, you are added to the friend list and can chat via Messages.

messages.title: Messages
messages.description: One-to-one chat with friends; send text, images, and files.

teamChat.title: Team Chat
teamChat.description: Group chat with all members of your organization. Only members of this organization can see and send messages.
teamChat.noMessages: No messages yet. Send the first one.
teamChat.selectOrgOrMember: Select an organization or be a member of this organization.

guide.title: User Guide
guide.subtitle: Learn how to use the RegLens website step by step.
guide.gettingStartedText: Go to the Sign up page to create an account. You can log in with email or username and password. After login you are redirected to the Dashboard.
guide.generalFlowText: In RegLens the workflow is: first add regulatory sources, the system detects updates; then map them to controls, generate tasks, collect evidence and finally export with audit packs.
guide.sidebarSectionText: After logging in you can reach all pages from the sidebar. Press ⌘K (or Ctrl+K) to open global search.
guide.addSourceText: To monitor regulatory content, first define a source. On the Sources page use "Add Source" to enter name and URL (RSS or API); after saving you can run a manual poll with "Poll Now".
guide.updateToTaskText: Select a change from the Updates page. On the detail page map to controls (AI suggestion or manual). Create a task from the mapping, assign it, add evidence and include in an audit pack if needed.
guide.notificationsSearchText: Use the bell icon in the top right for assigned tasks and other notifications. Global search (⌘K) lets you search updates, tasks, controls or frameworks and go to the relevant page. Use the Recent Activity section on the Dashboard to track latest actions.
guide.menuDashboard: Dashboard: Metrics, risk distribution, recent updates and critical tasks.
guide.menuSources: Sources: Add, edit and poll RSS/API sources.
guide.menuUpdates: Updates: Detected regulatory changes; filter by risk and status.
guide.menuControls: Controls: Framework controls and mappings to updates.
guide.menuTasks: Tasks: Generated tasks; assignment, priority and due date.
guide.menuEvidence: Evidence: Evidence templates and linking to tasks.
guide.menuAuditPacks: Audit Packs: Bundle tasks and evidence, export as PDF/Excel/CSV.
guide.menuFrameworks: Frameworks / Compliance / Security: Frameworks, compliance overview and security settings.
